note: several directive lines stacked after the diagram are each consumed+ignored; the single edge survives
control: an id that merely STARTS with a directive keyword (`styleA`) is a real node, NOT a consumed directive — the keyword must be a whole token
control: an edge endpoint id that starts with a directive keyword (`classroom`) is a real edge, not a consumed `class` directive
the >] shape strips surrounding double quotes (quote='"', content-exclusive label) just like the bracket shapes
the >] shape strips surrounding single quotes (quote="'", content-exclusive label)
control: an unquoted >] label still parses as the raw run (quote='') — only the quoted forms strip quotes
the mixed dash+pipe form `--|label|-->` is non-standard; it must WARN (no block / no nodes+edges), NOT silently succeed with the pipe chars leaked into the label value
thick analogue `==|label|==>` of the mixed dash+pipe form must WARN, not leak the pipe chars into the label as a false-green
control: the canonical pipe-label form `-->|label|` strips the pipes → label='label'
control: the canonical inline-dash form `-- label -->` yields label='label'
a longer closing shaft `--->` is one arrow; the extra shaft chars must NOT leak into the label (label='text', not 'text -')
closing shaft `---->` consumed whole; label stays 'text' (was leaking 'text --')
any shaft length closes cleanly; label stays 'text' (was leaking 'text ---')
thick: longer closing shaft `===>` is one arrow; label stays 'text' (was leaking 'text =')
thick: closing shaft `====>` consumed whole; label stays 'text' (was leaking 'text ==')
a double-quoted inline-dash label strips the surrounding quotes → label='q label' (mirrors the pipe form)
a single-quoted inline-dash label strips the surrounding quotes → label='q label'
thick: a double-quoted inline label strips the surrounding quotes → label='q label'
internal hyphens inside the label are preserved — only a shaft run that reaches `>` is a terminator
an internal pipe (not the FIRST char) is preserved as label content; only a leading `|` declines to the pipe-label form
control: the canonical pipe-label form `-->|label|` strips the pipes → label='label'
an asymmetric label may not begin with an unquoted opening bracket; `A>(text)]` must WARN/refuse, not corrupt to label '(text)'
`A>{x}]` (asymmetric, unquoted leading brace) must WARN/refuse, not corrupt to label '{x}'
control: a QUOTED asymmetric label may contain a leading bracket → label='(text)', quotes stripped
a bare hyphenated id is truncated to the kept prefix `receive`; the node span ends at the kept id so a span-rename leaves the discarded tail alone (slice===id)
a bare (unlabeled) thick arrow with a 3-char shaft parses to one edge A->B, like the bare dash long-shaft form; the extra `=` is a length hint, not a parse error
a bare thick arrow with a 4-char shaft parses to one edge A->B; any shaft length closes cleanly
the compact (no-space) bare long thick arrow also parses to one edge A->B
a real block mixing a normal arrow with a long thick arrow keeps BOTH edges; neither line fails the whole block
control: a headless thick link `===` (no `>`) still parses to one edge — the long-thick-arrow path does not shadow the open-link path

a reserved keyword used as an edge source (`style[Label] --> B`, off-contract) no longer lets the directive rule swallow the rest of the line: the same-line destination node B survives instead of vanishing; the reserved-source edge is dropped per the reserved-id limitation
same as the dash form for a thick arrow: the destination B is not silently lost when a reserved keyword leads an edge statement
